title: Ink and Bone
author: Rachel Caine
description: The first Great Library novel: in an alternate world where the Library of Alexandria never burned, owning a private book is a capital crime — and a young smuggler gets drafted into the Library's service.

title: Glass Houses
author: Rachel Caine
description: The first Morganville Vampires novel: a brilliant teenager at a Texas college discovers the town is secretly run by vampires — and her roommates' protection may not be enough.

title: Working Stiff
author: Rachel Caine
description: The first Revivalist novel: a Dallas zombie reanimation specialist is murdered on the job and reanimates herself — and must survive long enough to solve her own killing.

title: Stillhouse Lake
author: Rachel Caine
description: The ex-wife of a convicted serial killer tries to build a new life for her kids in an isolated lake community — and discovers her past has followed her there.

title: Undead and Unwed
author: MaryJanice Davidson
description: The first Betsy the Vampire Queen novel: a shoe-obsessed secretary is killed in a car accident, wakes up as a vampire, and discovers she is the prophesied queen of the undead.

title: Undead and Unemployed
author: MaryJanice Davidson
description: Queen Betsy #2: Betsy tries to hold down a retail job at a Macy's designer shoe department while balancing her vampire queen duties and a serial killer targeting the undead.

title: Derik's Bane
author: MaryJanice Davidson
description: A werewolf enforcer of the Wyndham pack is sent to kill a mysterious woman in California — who turns out to be the most powerful witch on the West Coast.

title: Greywalker
author: Kat Richardson
description: The first Greywalker novel: a Seattle private investigator wakes from a two-minute death with the ability to walk in the Grey — the space between the living and the dead.

title: Poltergeist
author: Kat Richardson
description: Greywalker #2: Harper Blaine is hired to investigate a fraudulent poltergeist experiment at a Seattle university — and discovers it may be creating a real one.

title: Underground
author: Kat Richardson
description: Greywalker #3: a rash of killings in Seattle's homeless community leads Harper into the city's buried underground — and its old, angry ghosts.

title: Touch the Dark
author: Karen Chance
description: The first Cassandra Palmer novel: a clairvoyant raised by the vampire mafia goes on the run when her powers attract attention from vampires, the Pythia, and a nasty mage.

title: Claimed by Shadow
author: Karen Chance
description: Cassandra Palmer #2: Cassie must figure out her new role as Pythia — chief oracle of the supernatural world — while three men vie for her formal binding.

title: Midnight's Daughter
author: Karen Chance
description: The first Dorina Basarab novel: Dracula's daughter, a half-vampire bounty hunter, is hired to recover her father who has escaped confinement in the supernatural underworld.

title: Rosemary's Baby
author: Ira Levin
description: Levin's landmark occult horror: a young woman in 1960s Manhattan moves into an old apartment building, becomes pregnant, and slowly realizes her neighbors have plans for her child.

title: The Stepford Wives
author: Ira Levin
description: A woman moving with her husband to a picture-perfect Connecticut suburb realizes the local housewives are just a little too happy to cook and clean — and a little too blank.

title: A Kiss Before Dying
author: Ira Levin
description: Levin's debut: a young college man has plans to marry into money — and will do anything, to any woman, to get there. The first novel to win the Edgar Award for Best First Novel.

title: The Boys from Brazil
author: Ira Levin
description: A Nazi hunter discovers a former SS doctor in Paraguay is coordinating the murders of 94 65-year-old men across Europe — and uncovers the real reason why.

title: The Other
author: Thomas Tryon
description: Tryon's novel of twin boys on a 1935 Connecticut farm whose summer is haunted by a series of accidents — and whose dead mother may be speaking through one of them.

title: Harvest Home
author: Thomas Tryon
description: A Manhattan couple moves to the perfect New England village of Cornwall Coombe — and discovers the town practices a pre-Christian fertility religion the matriarchs intend to continue.

title: The Exorcist
author: William Peter Blatty
description: Blatty's landmark horror: a Washington actress's 12-year-old daughter begins exhibiting symptoms no doctor can explain — and a Jesuit priest is called in to consider demonic possession.

title: Legion
author: William Peter Blatty
description: A sequel to The Exorcist: twelve years after Regan's exorcism, a series of ritualistic murders in Georgetown leads a detective to a psychiatric hospital — and to a patient who should be dead.

title: Suffer the Children
author: John Saul
description: Saul's debut: a New England town that once saw a brutal child-killing is now seeing it happen again — and a family's buried secret may be the key to stopping it.

title: Punish the Sinners
author: John Saul
description: Saul's Oregon small-town horror: a young priest arriving in a strict Catholic community discovers a pattern of teenage-girl suicides going back over a century.

title: The God Project
author: John Saul
description: Children in a small American town are dying of a strange wasting illness that resists every medical explanation — except one their parents refuse to believe.

title: The Family Fang
author: Kevin Wilson
description: Wilson's debut: the grown children of a famously eccentric performance-art couple come home to confront the people who grew them up as props in their art.

title: Nothing to See Here
author: Kevin Wilson
description: A woman is hired by her wealthy college roommate to care for her husband's twin children — who spontaneously combust when they get upset.

title: Now Is Not the Time to Panic
author: Kevin Wilson
description: Two outcast teenagers in a small Tennessee town in the summer of 1996 create a mysterious poster that accidentally triggers a nationwide panic.

title: Run for the Hills
author: Kevin Wilson
description: Wilson's latest: three half-siblings who have never met decide to track down their vanished father and drive across the country to find him.

title: Heroes Die
author: Matthew Stover
description: The first Acts of Caine novel: an Earth entertainment star whose fictional persona is an actual fantasy-world assassin must rescue his estranged wife from the same world he's been killing in for years.

title: Blade of Tyshalle
author: Matthew Stover
description: Acts of Caine #2: Hari Michaelson, crippled in the events of Heroes Die, navigates an Earth bureaucracy while his alter ego Caine's fantasy world faces genocidal invasion.

title: Caine Black Knife
author: Matthew Stover
description: Acts of Caine #3: Caine returns to the fantasy world of his breakout film to finally confront what he did on that original mission twenty years ago.

title: Caine's Law
author: Matthew Stover
description: Acts of Caine #4: the conclusion of Stover's grimdark saga as Caine must choose between his Earth family and the fantasy world that has become his conscience.

title: Taliesin
author: Stephen R. Lawhead
description: The first Pendragon Cycle novel: a Welsh bard and an Atlantean princess conceive the boy who will become Merlin — the grandfather of King Arthur.

title: Merlin
author: Stephen R. Lawhead
description: Pendragon Cycle #2: the life of Merlin from his Druid boyhood through his madness and the raising of Arthur — Lawhead's Celtic-historical Arthurian.

title: Arthur
author: Stephen R. Lawhead
description: Pendragon Cycle #3: the reign of Arthur as told by three successive narrators — Pelleas, Bedwyr, and Aneirin — the most ambitious volume of the cycle.

title: Byzantium
author: Stephen R. Lawhead
description: An Irish monk carrying the Book of Kells to the Byzantine emperor is shipwrecked and sold into slavery — and his journey through the medieval world becomes a spiritual odyssey.

title: Green Rider
author: Kristen Britain
description: The first Green Rider novel: a schoolgirl fleeing expulsion encounters a dying king's messenger and inherits his mission to carry a critical message across the kingdom.

title: First Rider's Call
author: Kristen Britain
description: Green Rider #2: Karigan G'ladheon returns to Sacoridia and finds the magical wall that has protected the kingdom for centuries beginning to fail.

title: The High King's Tomb
author: Kristen Britain
description: Green Rider #3: Karigan confronts an awakening ancient evil while the kingdom is plagued by a series of magical thefts from its most sacred burial site.

title: Dragon Prince
author: Melanie Rawn
description: The first Dragon Prince novel: a young prince who refuses to hunt dragons inherits his throne and must navigate a political minefield to marry his prophesied consort.

title: The Star Scroll
author: Melanie Rawn
description: Dragon Prince #2: the discovery of a lost star scroll threatens to return ancient dangerous sorcery to the Desert kingdoms.

title: Sunrunner's Fire
author: Melanie Rawn
description: Dragon Prince #3: the conclusion of the original trilogy as Andry of Graypearl rises to claim his destiny as Lord of Goddess Keep.

title: Archangel
author: Sharon Shinn
description: The first Samaria novel: on a world where angels rule as God's intermediaries, the reluctant next Archangel must find his prophesied human bride among the slave caste.

title: Jovah's Angel
author: Sharon Shinn
description: Samaria #2: 150 years after Archangel, a hurt angel and an engineer investigate why the god Jovah no longer seems to answer the angels' songs.

title: The Alleluia Files
author: Sharon Shinn
description: Samaria #3: a group of heretics searches for ancient data files that may reveal the true nature of Jovah — and shatter the theocratic order of the angels.

title: Myrren's Gift
author: Fiona McIntosh
description: The first Quickening trilogy: a young Morgravian general inherits a dying witch's strange gift and finds himself able to trade bodies with another man at will.

title: Blood and Memory
author: Fiona McIntosh
description: The Quickening #2: Wyl Thirsk uses the witch's gift to survive assassination attempt after assassination attempt, each time waking in another body.

title: Bridge of Souls
author: Fiona McIntosh
description: The Quickening #3: the final confrontation with the king who betrayed Wyl and the reckoning with the witch's curse that has preserved him.

title: Night of Knives
author: Ian Cameron Esslemont
description: The first Malazan Empire novel: set on the island of Malaz on the Shadow Moon night, a young initiate witnesses the event that will reshape the empire's leadership.

title: Return of the Crimson Guard
author: Ian Cameron Esslemont
description: Malazan Empire #2: the ancient mercenary Crimson Guard return to Quon Tali after their century-long vow is fulfilled — and find the empire they vowed to destroy greatly changed.

title: Stonewielder
author: Ian Cameron Esslemont
description: Malazan Empire #3: on the Korel subcontinent, a long-isolated Malazan expedition must navigate the local gods' political crises and the return of the ancient Stormriders.

title: Hidden Empire
author: Kevin J. Anderson
description: The first Saga of Seven Suns novel: humanity's stellar civilization accidentally ignites a gas giant — waking a species of beings who consider the act a declaration of war.

title: A Forest of Stars
author: Kevin J. Anderson
description: Saga of Seven Suns #2: the Hydrogues' war against organic life spreads across the galaxy as human, Ildiran, and tree-priest factions struggle to mount a response.

title: Horizon Storms
author: Kevin J. Anderson
description: Saga of Seven Suns #3: the war expands as other elemental races join the fight, and the secrets of the ancient Klikiss robots begin to come out.

title: Climbing Olympus
author: Kevin J. Anderson
description: Anderson's Mars-colonization novel: an 'augie' — a bioengineered settler — climbs Olympus Mons in search of the mystery that has been killing her kind.

title: The Summoner
author: Gail Z. Martin
description: The first Chronicles of the Necromancer: a young prince with the gift of speaking to the dead must flee his murdering brother and raise an army of spirits against him.

title: The Blood King
author: Gail Z. Martin
description: Chronicles of the Necromancer #2: Prince Tris Drayke gathers allies among the dead as his evil brother Jared consolidates power and courts a vampire ally.

title: Dark Haven
author: Gail Z. Martin
description: Chronicles of the Necromancer #3: King Martris Drayke rebuilds his war-torn kingdom while the last of Jared's magic reaches for him from the grave.

title: Hospital Station
author: James White
description: The first Sector General novel: linked stories of a gigantic multispecies hospital in deep space where the medical staff must learn to treat patients whose biology they've never seen.

title: Star Surgeon
author: James White
description: Sector General #2: Dr. Conway must handle a new crisis on a plague-planet while the hospital itself becomes the target of a military strike.

title: Major Operation
author: James White
description: Sector General #3: linked stories in which Dr. Conway investigates a world where the entire planet turns out to be a single vast organism.

title: Ventus
author: Karl Schroeder
description: A planet with terraforming AIs that have gone sentient and begun worshipping the long-dead human engineers who built them — Schroeder's transhuman SF debut.

title: Permanence
author: Karl Schroeder
description: An orphan pilot on a dead backwater colony stumbles into a mystery involving an ancient galactic civilization whose successors still walk among humanity.

title: Sun of Suns
author: Karl Schroeder
description: The first Virga novel: Schroeder's space opera set inside a giant balloon-world where towns rotate for gravity and nations wage wars between artificial suns.

title: Inkheart
author: Cornelia Funke
description: The first Inkworld novel: a father with the power to read characters out of their books accidentally brings a villain into our world — and loses his wife into the villain's book.

title: Inkspell
author: Cornelia Funke
description: Inkworld #2: Meggie and her father Mo are drawn into the Inkworld itself — the book whose fictional characters they've been freeing.

title: Inkdeath
author: Cornelia Funke
description: Inkworld #3: trapped inside the Inkworld as the evil Adderhead ravages the land, Meggie's family must write a new ending for a story that has become real.

title: The Thief Lord
author: Cornelia Funke
description: Two runaway brothers hiding in Venice fall in with a charismatic teenage thief who calls himself the Thief Lord — and who has a magical secret.

title: The Alchemyst
author: Michael Scott
description: The first Secrets of the Immortal Nicholas Flamel: teenage twins discover their bookseller employer is the immortal Nicholas Flamel, and they are the prophesied twins of legend.

series.name: The Secrets of the Immortal Nicholas Flamel

title: The Magician
author: Michael Scott
description: Nicholas Flamel #2: the twins travel to Paris to meet Niccolò Machiavelli and the Comte de Saint-Germain while Dr. John Dee hunts them across time.

series.name: The Secrets of the Immortal Nicholas Flamel

title: The Sorceress
author: Michael Scott
description: Nicholas Flamel #3: the twins travel to London to find the sorceress Gilgamesh and unlock the Book of Abraham's final secrets before Dee can destroy them.

series.name: The Secrets of the Immortal Nicholas Flamel

title: Mortal Engines
author: Philip Reeve
description: The first Mortal Engines novel: in a post-apocalyptic world of traction cities that roll across the land consuming smaller settlements, an apprentice historian falls out of London.

title: Predator's Gold
author: Philip Reeve
description: Mortal Engines #2: Tom and Hester flee to the ice-skating predator city of Anchorage as the Green Storm continues its war against the Traction Cities.

title: Infernal Devices
author: Philip Reeve
description: Mortal Engines #3: fifteen years later, Tom and Hester's daughter Wren is kidnapped by Lost Boys — and the fate of the Anchorage community is decided.

title: A Darkling Plain
author: Philip Reeve
description: Mortal Engines #4: the final confrontation with the Stalker Fang as the Traction Cities and Green Storm are both destroyed by a returning ancient weapon.

title: The Ear, the Eye, and the Arm
author: Nancy Farmer
description: In a 2194 Zimbabwe, three children of the military general are kidnapped and hunted by three mutant detectives with uncannily sharpened senses.

title: The House of the Scorpion
author: Nancy Farmer
description: In the future country of Opium, a boy raised for his organs realizes he is the clone of the 140-year-old drug lord El Patrón — and decides not to die for him.

title: A Girl Named Disaster
author: Nancy Farmer
description: An 11-year-old Mozambican girl flees an arranged marriage and crosses the African wilderness alone — Farmer's Newbery Honor coming-of-age adventure.

title: Winterlong
author: Elizabeth Hand
description: Hand's debut: in a post-apocalyptic Washington DC ruled by decadent aesthetes, a young empath is drawn into a plot that could bring back the old gods.

title: Waking the Moon
author: Elizabeth Hand
description: A young woman at a Catholic university in the 1970s stumbles onto an ancient moon-goddess cult whose members are gathering for an astrological ritual twenty years later.

title: Generation Loss
author: Elizabeth Hand
description: The first Cass Neary novel: a washed-out 1970s punk photographer travels to coastal Maine to interview a reclusive female art photographer — and walks into a disappearance.

title: Lost Souls
author: Poppy Z. Brite
description: Brite's debut: three decadent vampires on a road trip through the American South encounter a young runaway with his own supernatural heritage.

title: Drawing Blood
author: Poppy Z. Brite
description: A comic-book artist returning to his hometown and a hacker on the run from the FBI find themselves trapped in a haunted house at the edge of the Missing Mile.

title: Exquisite Corpse
author: Poppy Z. Brite
description: Two serial killers — one English, one American — find each other through a personals ad in New Orleans and become grotesquely in love.

title: The Priest of Blood
author: Douglas Clegg
description: The first Vampyricon novel: a medieval peasant huntsman is taken by vampire lords and sent on a quest to find a prophesied relic across a dying Europe.

title: Neverland
author: Douglas Clegg
description: Clegg's debut: a group of cousins spending summer at their grandmother's Georgia island discover a dark magical world ruled by their strange younger cousin.

title: The Halloween Man
author: Douglas Clegg
description: A woman returns to her hometown to confront the urban legend of a man who takes children on Halloween — and who may be more than a legend.

title: The Isle of Glass
author: Judith Tarr
description: The first Hound and the Falcon novel: a young half-elven monk in a medieval English monastery must choose between his vows and the elvenkind who claim him.

title: The Hall of the Mountain King
author: Judith Tarr
description: The first Avaryan Rising novel: a crown prince of a god-descended line must navigate political intrigue and magical destiny in Tarr's Byzantine-inspired fantasy.

title: A Fall of Princes
author: Judith Tarr
description: Avaryan Rising #3: the consolidation of the Sun-Son's empire through the eyes of an enemy prince — Tarr's most politically intricate volume.

title: So You Want to Be a Wizard
author: Diane Duane
description: The first Young Wizards novel: a bullied 13-year-old picks up a library book that is actually a wizarding manual and chooses to take the Oath.

title: Deep Wizardry
author: Diane Duane
description: Young Wizards #2: Nita and Kit join a group of cetacean wizards to perform a ritual that will save the coast — and that may require a human sacrifice.

title: High Wizardry
author: Diane Duane
description: Young Wizards #3: Nita's little sister Dairine receives her own wizard's manual and takes a wild interplanetary tour while Nita and Kit race to catch up.

title: The Door into Fire
author: Diane Duane
description: The first Tale of the Five novel: a young prince who can summon fire elementals searches across the land for his missing lover — Duane's adult fantasy debut.
